overall_justification: While the article focuses on Japan's historical monetary policy shifts, its framing and conclusion emphasize the dangers of currency instability and the benefits of a stable gold standard.  The narrative implicitly criticizes policies that "break the standard" and praises the "stability of the standard of value" achieved through adherence to gold. This aligns with the Orthodox perspective in the Brazilian context, which prioritizes a strong, stable currency linked to gold.

supporting_evidence.quote: Pela lei de 1897, realizou-se de metade o peso do ouro e a metade de 1 yen, ouro, e 2 fun (11.57 graos) foi fixado como peso da nova moeda de 1 yen, ouro. Em outras palavras, quebrou-se o padrão...
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote highlights a critical moment where Japan 'broke the standard,' a concept often criticized by Orthodox thinkers in Brazil who feared similar actions.

supporting_evidence.quote: Devido á estabilidade do padrão do valor, em consequencia da adopção do padrão ouro, todas as fluctuações referentes aos preços dos productos são regulados pela relação normal da oferta e procura...
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote emphasizes the benefits of a gold standard, aligning with the core argument of the Orthodox perspective in the Brazilian debate.

supporting_evidence.quote: Esta estabilidade no padrão do valor introduziu ordem e systema nas transacções commerciaes, tanto domesticas, como estrangeiras.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This reinforces the perceived positive outcomes of a stable gold standard, a key argument used by Orthodox proponents in Brazil.
